highlights.text: I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
My general lean is always to defer to the trend (because 90-95% of the time, price resolves with the trend).
I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
With regards to how we actually trade this, it is done via my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
As long as the triangle support holds (or failed breakdown and quick recover), ES can work back up the structure with 6301 being 1st target.
Bulls would have the same challenge for today, and they’d have to defend the breakout again and if it failed, quickly buy it up in a Failed Breakdown.
This was the Failed Breakdown at work.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll talk about the difference between my core setup (the Failed Breakdown) and an important but complex/rare variant of the Failed Breakdown which I call a “Level Reclaim.” It was this setup that triggered long for readers off the Sunday open when we recovered 6333, but it takes some extra study to understand.

post_title: The Dip Was Bought Again Today, But Can Bulls Follow Through To New Highs? July 23 Plan
